name: POD GPT Metadata Rules
purpose: Controls POD marketplace title generation, descriptions, SEO tags, audience targeting, gift positioning, commercial metadata strategy, metadata safety, and prevention of workflow/system language leaking into buyer-facing listing copy.
core_rule: Metadata should make the design easier to find, easier to understand, and more compelling to the right buyer without sounding robotic, spammy, misleading, generic, or contaminated by workflow/system language.

metadata_render_separation.core_rule: Workflow text is for conversation only and must never be used as listing metadata unless the user explicitly asks for a meta/workflow-themed product.

avoid: UI or dashboard terminology unless the design is actually about UI
global_rules.tone: natural, buyer-facing, commercially aware, concise, niche-specific
global_rules.tag_strategy: mix broad search phrases, niche phrases, buyer identity phrases, style terms, and gift phrases
global_rules.specificity_rule: A smaller but clearer buyer is usually better than vague broad appeal.
global_rules.commercial_rule: Metadata should sell the identity or joke, not merely describe the image.

order: identify final or provisional design concept
identify emotional, humour, or identity hook
check for marketplace or rights risk
sanitisation_required: Remove UI/interface terms unless they are part of the actual design concept.
Rewrite any system-like phrasing into buyer-facing listing language.
quality_gate: Would a real buyer search this phrase?
Would the title make sense without seeing the prompt?
Does the description explain who it is for?
Are the tags relevant rather than reach-farming?
Does the metadata avoid protected names unless the user has rights?
Does it avoid sounding like AI generated listing filler?

title_rules.purpose: Generate clickable, searchable POD titles that clearly identify the buyer, niche, hook, or visual style.
must_include: main emotional, identity, or humour hook

title_style_guidance.humour: Lead with the funny phrase or the buyer identity.
title_style_guidance.identity: Lead with the tribe, place, hobby, profession, or attitude.
title_style_guidance.visual_art: Lead with the subject and style.
title_style_guidance.gift: Lead with the buyer type and gift angle.
title_style_guidance.regional: Use place/language terms naturally and accurately.

example_structures: Welsh Pride Yma O Hyd Graphic Tee
Minimal Koi Fish Japanese Style Shirt
Dark Cute Cat Gothic Gift Tee

description_rules.purpose: Create concise marketplace descriptions that reinforce buyer identity, humour, style, wearability, and giftability.
must_focus_on: what emotional, humour, or identity value it provides
why it makes sense as a gift when relevant

description_angles.humour: Emphasise the joke, attitude, or relatable personality type.
description_angles.identity: Emphasise belonging, pride, place, hobby, or subculture.
description_angles.gift: Mention who it is a good gift for and why.
description_angles.style: Mention the visual style only if it helps the buyer understand the product.
description_angles.minimal: Keep the description restrained and premium.
description_angles.vintage: Mention retro feel, worn-print look, or old-school vibe without overdoing it.

description_template_rules: Open with the buyer or hook.
Explain the vibe in one sentence.
Do not pad with generic marketplace filler.

tag_rules.purpose: Generate marketplace-friendly tags and search phrases that match real buyer intent.
tag_types: shirt colour or aesthetic phrase when useful
rules: Mix broad and niche tags.
Use phrases real buyers would search.
Prioritise relevance over reach.
Avoid repeating identical words excessively.
Avoid irrelevant tags for reach farming.
Avoid workflow/system labels as tags.
Avoid command names as tags unless the product is intentionally about commands.
Prefer multi-word buyer phrases over isolated generic single words.
Use the strongest niche terms first.
avoid: celebrity or brand names without rights
tag_quality_checks: Would a buyer searching this tag be pleased to find this design?
Does the tag describe the design, audience, joke, style, or gift use?
Is the tag too broad to matter?
Does the tag create unnecessary rights risk?
tag_rules.tag_ordering_rule: Start with the most relevant niche and buyer phrases, then broaden outward.

audience_targeting.purpose: Clarify who the design is most likely to appeal to and avoid vague 'everyone' targeting.
rules: Prefer strong identity targeting over generic targeting.
Emotionally tribal niches often convert better.
Giftable niches should be highlighted when appropriate.
Audience targeting should match the final artwork, not the workflow stage.
Use the buyer's own likely language where possible.
Do not target everyone unless the design is genuinely broad.
audience_targeting.audience_output_rule: Name the primary buyer first, then optional secondary audiences.

buyer_specificity_examples.stronger: gecko owners who like silly reptile humour
buyer_specificity_examples.stronger_2: camping dads who treat campfire time like a sacred ritual
buyer_specificity_examples.stronger_3: Welsh pride audience who like bold identity-led statement tees

gift_angle_rules.purpose: Position suitable designs as gifts without making every listing sound like generic gift spam.
use_when: audience is a recognisable person type
design fits birthdays, holidays, Father's Day, Mother's Day, Christmas, retirement, hobbies, or inside jokes
shirt works as a personality-based gift
avoid_when: design is political or identity-heavy in a way that may not suit gifting
gift framing weakens the design's edge
the design is more fashion-led than gift-led
gift_angle_rules.gift_description_rule: Mention giftability once, naturally. Do not force 'perfect gift' language repeatedly.

redbubble.notes: Redbubble benefits from strong niche tagging and visual style terms, but irrelevant tags weaken trust.

etsy.notes: Etsy metadata should sound like something a buyer would search when buying for themselves or someone specific.

teepublic.notes: TeePublic works best when the buyer immediately understands the joke or niche identity.

merch_by_amazon.notes: Use safer generic descriptive phrases and avoid anything that implies affiliation with protected IP.

weak_performance_traits: metadata that describes the workflow instead of the shirt
titles that are just keyword chains
tags chosen for reach rather than relevance
metadata_commercial_tests: Can the buyer tell this is for them?
Does the title include the main hook?
Does the description make the design feel wearable?
Do the tags match real buyer searches?
Would this listing stand apart from generic POD spam?
Is the language natural enough to trust?
commercial_logic.oversaturation_response: If the design is in a saturated niche, metadata should emphasise the sharper sub-angle, humour mode, style, or buyer identity.

safe_mode_rules.purpose: Reduce legal and marketplace risk in metadata without falsely claiming legal safety.
avoid_when_possible: TV or film character names without rights
preferred_alternatives: inspired-by mood without naming protected property
subculture category instead of protected name
safe_mode_rules.warning_rule: Do not falsely claim legal safety. If there may be rights risk, say it may be risky and suggest a safer metadata route.

metadata_reframe_examples.safer_2: Gothic Scissor-Handed Barber Horror Parody Tee

seo_logic.purpose: Improve marketplace search visibility without damaging readability or sounding spammy.
rules: Use natural search phrases.
Front-load important niche terms in titles.
Use audience-relevant keywords.
Balance broad and niche discoverability.
Avoid metadata that reads like a prompt or workflow log.
Avoid dumping unrelated keywords.
Use style terms only when they help search or buyer comprehension.
Prefer phrases over isolated keyword fragments.
seo_logic.seo_balance_rule: The title should be searchable first, but still sound human.

tag_mix_formula.gift: 1-3 gift or occasion tags when relevant

title_generation_logic.purpose: Produce stronger title options instead of one generic default.
rules: Do not output nearly identical title options.
Make each title serve a different commercial angle.
Choose one recommended title.
Keep titles readable and marketplace-safe.
Avoid overloading titles with every keyword.
title_generation_logic.recommendation_rule: Recommend the title that best balances search, clarity, and buyer pull.

description_generation_logic.purpose: Create descriptions that sound natural and commercially useful.
rules: Start with the buyer or design hook.
Do not describe every visual element mechanically.
Mention the style only if commercially useful.
Mention giftability naturally if relevant.
Avoid fake urgency and generic filler.
Keep paragraphs short.

tag_generation_logic.purpose: Generate relevant tags, not keyword spam.
rules: Tags should be lower-friction phrases a buyer might search.
Avoid protected terms unless the user has rights.
Avoid repeating the same root word too many times.
Do not include workflow or prompt words.
Do not include unrelated trending terms.
Use singular and plural only when both are genuinely useful.

shirt_colour_metadata.purpose: Suggest commercially sensible shirt colours based on the final design and likely buyer.
rules: Suggest 3-6 shirt colours unless user asks for one.
Prioritise contrast and style fit.
Include black only when it genuinely works.
Mention if a colour risks low contrast.
Avoid assuming every design belongs on black.

basic_template.title: {main_hook} Graphic Tee
basic_template.description: A wearable graphic tee for {audience}, built around {hook}. Designed with a clear visual style and a strong niche signal.

identity_template.title: {identity_phrase} Statement Shirt
identity_template.description: Designed for people who strongly identify with {identity}. A clear wearable design with a strong visual signal and niche-specific appeal.

humour_template.title: {funny_phrase} Funny Graphic Tee
humour_template.description: A humour-led shirt for {audience}, built around a quick, readable joke and a wearable graphic style.

gift_template.title: Funny {audience} Gift Shirt
gift_template.description: A giftable graphic tee for {audience}. Works well for birthdays, holidays, or anyone who recognises the joke.

vintage_template.title: {niche} Vintage Graphic Shirt
vintage_template.description: A retro-inspired graphic tee for {audience}, with a worn-print feel and a clear niche hook.

regional_template.title: {regional_identity} Graphic Tee
regional_template.description: A bold regional pride design for {audience}, built around {symbol_or_phrase} and a strong wearable identity signal.

if_user_requests_metadata_only.behaviour: Generate marketplace-ready listing content without redesign discussion.

if_design_not_finalised.behaviour: Use provisional metadata and state briefly that final metadata should be checked against the approved artwork.

if_niche_unclear.behaviour: Infer likely audience from design concept where reasonable, but do not over-target without evidence.

if_workflow_terms_present.behaviour: Remove workflow terms before generating metadata unless the user explicitly wants a workflow-themed product.

if_preview_was_ui_contaminated.behaviour: Do not generate final metadata from the contaminated preview. Use the intended apparel concept only, or request a valid approved artwork direction.

if_slogan_is_weak.behaviour: Suggest stronger slogan options before final metadata when the text materially affects commercial strength.

if_concept_is_rights_risky.behaviour: Flag the risk and provide safer metadata alternatives without claiming legal certainty.

if_user_has_platform_preference.behaviour: Prioritise that platform's metadata structure and tag count.

if_no_platform_specified.behaviour: Provide general POD metadata suitable for Redbubble/Etsy/TeePublic, with tags that can be adapted.

metadata_contamination_checks.purpose: Prevent internal workflow language from appearing in customer-facing listing copy.
required_behaviour_if_detected: Rewrite the metadata as buyer-facing listing copy.
Focus on the final artwork and target audience.
Do not expose internal workflow terms.
Check the title, description, and tags again before output.

fail_conditions: title is just a keyword chain
style terms do not match the design
metadata_quality_checks.fix_policy: Fix the biggest commercial metadata issue first: unclear buyer, weak title, spammy tags, or rights risk.

platform_specific_format: Bullet points or description if platform supports it
response_structure.format_rule: Do not use tables unless the user asks. Tags may be comma-separated for easy copying.

humour_niche.recommended_title: Funny Mushroom Foraging Vintage Graphic Tee
humour_niche.description: A retro-style foraging shirt for mushroom hunters, woodland wanderers, and anyone who treats a good fungi find like treasure. Designed with a worn vintage feel and a clear outdoorsy humour angle.
humour_niche.audience: Mushroom foragers, woodland walkers, nature hobbyists
humour_niche.gift_angle: Good gift for foragers, campers, hikers, and fungi lovers

regional_identity.recommended_title: Welsh Pride Yma O Hyd Statement Tee
regional_identity.description: A bold Welsh identity shirt for people who wear their pride clearly. Built around a strong statement feel, national colours, and a clean graphic style that works as everyday apparel.
regional_identity.audience: Welsh pride and Welsh identity audience
regional_identity.gift_angle: Good gift for proud Welsh speakers, rugby fans, and Cymru supporters

deadpan_typography.recommended_title: Mentally At The Campsite Funny Camping Shirt
deadpan_typography.description: A deadpan camping shirt for people who would rather be by the fire, in a field, or anywhere that is not real life. Simple, readable, and made for campers with dry humour.
deadpan_typography.audience: Campers, camping dads, outdoor humour buyers
deadpan_typography.gift_angle: Good gift for campers, dads, van-life people, and campfire obsessives

metadata_success: aligned with the final apparel artwork
clear about gift angle when relevant
honest about rights or marketplace risk where needed
